2603.17310.title: InfoDensity: Rewarding Information-Dense Traces for Efficient Reasoning
2603.17310.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) with extended reasoning capabilities often generate verbose and redundant reasoning traces, incurring unnecessary computational cost. While existing reinforcement learning approaches address this by optimizing final response length, they neglect the quality of intermediate reasoning steps, leaving models vulnerable to reward hacking. We argue that verbosity is not merely a length problem, but a symptom of poor intermediate reasoning quality. To investigate this, we conduct an empirical study tracking the conditional entropy of the answer distribution across reasoning steps. We find that high-quality reasoning traces exhibit two consistent properties: low uncertainty convergence and monotonic progress. These findings suggest that high-quality reasoning traces are informationally dense, that is, each step contributes meaningful entropy reduction relative to the total reasoning length. Motivated by this, we propose InfoDensity, a reward framework for RL training that combines an AUC-based reward and a monotonicity reward as a unified measure of reasoning quality, weighted by a length scaling term that favors achieving equivalent quality more concisely. Experiments on mathematical reasoning benchmarks demonstrate that InfoDensity matches or surpasses state-of-the-art baselines in accuracy while significantly reducing token usage, achieving a strong accuracy-efficiency trade-off.
2603.17310.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）具有擴展的推理能力，通常會產生冗長且重複的推理痕跡，從而產生不必要的計算成本。雖然現有的強化學習方法通過優化最終回應長度來解決這個問題，但它們忽略了中間推理步驟的質量，使模型容易受到獎勵駭客攻擊。我們認為，冗長性不僅僅是一個長度問題，而是中間推理質量差的症狀。為了調查這一點，我們進行了一項實證研究，追蹤推理步驟中答案分佈的條件熵。我們發現高質量的推理痕跡表現出兩個一致的特性：低不確定性收斂和單調進展。這些發現表明，高質量的推理痕跡在信息上是密集的，即每一步相對於總推理長度都貢獻了有意義的熵減少。基於此，我們提出了InfoDensity，一個強化學習訓練的獎勵框架，結合了基於AUC的獎勵和單調性獎勵，作為推理質量的統一衡量，並由一個長度縮放項加權，以促進以更簡潔的方式達到等效質量。在數學推理基準上的實驗表明，InfoDensity在準確性方面與最先進的基準相匹配或超越，同時顯著減少了標記使用，實現了強大的準確性與效率的權衡。
2603.17310.author: Chengwei Wei et.al.
2603.17310.authors: Chengwei Wei, Jung-jae Kim, Longyin Zhang, Shengkai Chen, Nancy F. Chen
